extraction.one_line_summary: GAUDU David surged in final 300m after poor positioning before climb, winning by using climbing strength to recover and then accelerate late on uphill finish.
tactical_patterns: GAUDU recovered from pre-climb positional error by using climbing superiority to not just rejoin but counterattack in final 200m.
VILLELLA Davide led out finale but faded final 100m, suggesting Astana misjudged pacing on steep gradient kick.
Multiple attacks inside final 3km (THOMAS Benjamin, others) failed because speeds too high before base climb positioning critical.

rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: Won after poor positioning shows superior climbing acceleration on 300m uphill finishes can overcome tactical errors
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Retained yellow by only 6 seconds despite stage placing, indicating limited time gap defense on punchy finishes
rider_signals.COSTA Rui: positive: Third at 8 seconds shows strong positioning and sprint on steep uphill finales for classics-type rider
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: Fifth place suggests struggled to match final acceleration despite being on GAUDU's wheel in final 200m
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Dropped to fifth GC at 22 seconds after initiating early attacks that failed, wasting energy before decisive moment
rider_signals.VILLELLA Davide: negative: Led finale but faded badly suggests Astana's pacing strategy poor on variable gradient punchy climbs
